title: A vállalkozások fenntartható tevékenysége. A taxonómiarendelet hatásainak piacelméleti modellezése
summary_en: What economic effects will the EU's Taxonomy Regulation have on Hungarian firms — particularly the SMEs that are currently outside the disclosure mandate? Böcskei and Somogyi build a market-theory game-theoretic model to compare three regimes: no regulation, the current rule (large firms only), and a future expanded rule (SMEs included). The model evaluates each regime against social-welfare criteria. Findings highlight the trade-off between disclosure-induced incentives for genuine sustainable activity and the compliance costs imposed on firms. Particularly relevant given that the European Commission is actively considering extending the disclosure regime to SMEs, which could substantially reshape the competitive structure of Hungarian green-product markets.
data_used: Theoretical paper. Game-theoretic model of firm behaviour under three regulatory regimes for sustainability disclosure. No empirical contribution.
policy_relevance: Direct relevance to Hungarian implementation of the EU Taxonomy Regulation (NGM, MNB, AM). The paper supports careful welfare evaluation before extending the disclosure regime to SMEs — particularly because compliance costs are non-trivial and Hungarian SME capacity for ESG reporting is uneven. Useful framework for the Hungarian negotiating position in EU-level discussions.
